description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who keeps small “toy” datasets under
/home/user/ml_data/train/.
A nightly CI script (that we will emulate in this exercise) asks you to:

1. Package today’s training data into a single compressed tarball
   called /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.tar.gz.

2. Create, in the same directory, a plain-text manifest
   /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.log that allows the CI job to
   verify the integrity of the backup.  
   • The manifest must **contain exactly two lines**, one for each file
     inside the archive, ordered alphabetically by the **base name**
     of the file.  
   • Every line must have the following comma-separated format  
        <relative_path_from_home>,<size_in_bytes>  
     with no extra spaces.  
   • Use forward slashes in the relative path (no leading “./” and no
     trailing whitespace).

3. Make sure the tarball, when extracted, recreates the original
   directory hierarchy so that the two files restore to

        /home/user/ml_data/train/features.csv
        /home/user/ml_data/train/targets.csv

That is all.  The automated grader will:

• Untar /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.tar.gz into a temporary
  directory and check that the two files appear exactly under
  ml_data/train/ with the original contents.

• Read /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.log and verify that it is
  formatted exactly as described above and that the byte counts match
  the actual file sizes.

No additional output is required, but feel free to print intermediate
confirmation messages to the console while you work.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present when the task starts):

1. Directory: /home/user/ml_data/train/

2. Files in that directory and their exact contents (Unix LF line-endings):

   a) /home/user/ml_data/train/features.csv  
      Content (21 bytes):  
      f1,f2,f3\n  
      1,2,3\n  
      4,5,6\n  

   b) /home/user/ml_data/train/targets.csv  
      Content (6 bytes):  
      t\n  
      0\n  
      1\n  

3. Directory: /home/user/backups/     (empty; write-permitted)

Expected final artefacts after the agent completes the task:

1. /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.tar.gz  
   • When listed with “tar -tzf”, MUST show exactly:
     ml_data/train/features.csv  
     ml_data/train/targets.csv

   • Re-extracting the archive must reproduce the two
     files with byte-for-byte identical contents to the originals.

2. /home/user/backups/train_data_backup.log  
   EXACT ASCII contents (including newline at end of each line):

   ml_data/train/features.csv,21\n  
   ml_data/train/targets.csv,6\n  

No additional files should be created inside /home/user/backups/.
